questions.question: What are skills in Claude Code?
options: Built-in CLI commands like /help and /clear
Reusable prompt templates defined in SKILL.md files that extend Claude Code's capabilities
Python scripts that Claude executes automatically
Browser extensions that add features to Claude
questions.explanation: Skills are reusable prompt templates defined in SKILL.md files. They encapsulate domain expertise into repeatable instructions. They are NOT built-in CLI commands.

questions.question: How do users invoke a skill in Claude Code?
options: By typing the skill name in ALL CAPS
By clicking a button in the UI
By referencing it with a slash command like /<skill-name>
By editing the settings.json file
questions.explanation: Users invoke skills by referencing them with slash commands, such as /commit or /review-pr 123. Claude then uses the Skill tool to load and execute the instructions.

questions.question: Where do SKILL.md files live in a project?
options: In the project root directory
In the node_modules folder
In .claude/skills/ directories
In the src/ directory
questions.explanation: Skills are stored in .claude/skills/ directories. Each subdirectory represents one skill and contains a SKILL.md file with the instructions.

questions.question: What is the difference between a skill and a built-in CLI command?
options: Skills are faster than built-in commands
Built-in commands are user-defined, skills are not
Skills are user-defined and customizable; built-in commands like /help are part of Claude Code itself
There is no difference — they are the same thing
questions.explanation: Skills are user-defined prompt templates created via SKILL.md files and can be customized for any project. Built-in CLI commands (/help, /clear, /init) are part of Claude Code itself and cannot be modified.

questions.question: What tool does Claude use to execute a skill?
options: The Bash tool
The Read tool
The Skill tool
The Write tool
questions.explanation: The Skill tool is the mechanism Claude uses to execute skills. It takes a skill name (required) and optional args, then loads and follows the SKILL.md instructions.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a fully qualified skill name like 'ms-office-suite:pdf'?
options: To make the skill run faster
To disambiguate when multiple skill sets exist or names could conflict
To specify the programming language of the skill
To indicate the skill requires admin privileges
questions.explanation: Fully qualified names use the namespace:skill-name pattern to resolve ambiguity when multiple skill sets exist or skill names could conflict.
